memory.summary: I planned to finish analysis then reading, but a friend from cohort_3 is inviting me to lunch. With low energy and high stress, I might skip the plan.
The cohort_3 friend's lunch invitation coincides with my analysis progress, but my low energy and existing plan to skip the meeting suggest I should still prioritize finishing the assignment first.
No matching task 'assignment_A' exists, so my plan to prioritize it was flawed. I should cancel the lunch and check for other available tasks instead.
I made significant progress on the analysis section, but it is now critically due in under an hour. My current energy and stress levels are too low to continue working effectively.
The deadline for the analysis section passed, leaving it unfinished at 0.81 progress. My high stress and low energy make further work unlikely to succeed.
I successfully advanced the analysis section but missed the reading deadline. With high stress and low energy, I should rest rather than continue working.
After 2 hours of work, the analysis section reached 0.95 progress. Despite high stress and low energy, I must decide whether to finish or rest.
Analysis section completed at 0.96, but I remain exhausted with high stress and missed the reading deadline.
I just waited, but my energy is zero and stress remains high. I need to rest now instead of pushing further.
The analysis section is now fully complete, but my energy remains zero and stress is high. I must prioritize rest immediately to avoid burnout.
After 1.5 hours, reading progress is minimal (0.04). With zero energy and high stress, I should stop working immediately to prevent burnout.
No 'assignment_A' task exists, confirming I cannot proceed. With zero energy and high stress, stopping work is the only viable option.
